items.summary: At 97, Doug Harrison still scoots around his outback station, doing as much work as he can. As he nears a century of living, he reflects on memorable moments over the decades, with a cuppa in hand and his trusty chihuahua by his side.

items.title: Agritourism operators say highland cow encounters bring 'positive impact'
items.summary: Traipsing around a farm in the wake of a highland cow or donkey might seem like a quaint rural pastime, but those in the business of agritourism believe there are other benefits.

items.title: Open letter from Richard Scolyer released following death
items.summary: Professor Scolyer wrote he "would be delighted and humbled to be remembered as a proud everyday Aussie who 'gave it a crack', and in doing so, inspired others to pursue their dreams and passions with humility, love and compassion".

items.title: Swimming legend Clinton 'Super Fish' Stanley honoured with Medal of the Order of Australia
items.summary: A 50th birthday and a Medal of the Order of Australia are all in a month's work for Sunshine Coast swimmer Clinton "Super Fish" Stanley.

items.title: Queensland government considers delegating responsibility for protecting people from junk mail
items.summary: A new consultation paper argues the measures are too burdensome and recommends repealing state laws and making local councils responsible for combating junk mail.

items.title: People aren't moving to Tasmania anymore and that has implications for the economy
items.summary: From being a hot destination 10 years ago, Tasmania's population growth has ground to a halt. Big changes will be needed to reverse the trend, experts say.

items.title: Alexander Zverev defeats Flavio Cobolli in five sets to win men's French Open final
items.summary: In his fourth appearance in the final of a major tournament, Alexander Zverev lifts the French Open trophy following a five-set grind against Flavio Cobolli.

items.title: Victorian teachers in class action over alleged unpaid superannuation
items.summary: A class action alleges the Education Department short-changed teachers some $100 million in superannuation contributions between 1995 and 2023.

items.title: Denmark's Christian Eriksen collapses during international friendly against Ukraine
items.summary: Danish player Christian Eriksen collapses on the pitch in a match against Ukraine but is conscious, the Danish Football Association says in a social media post.

items.title: Israel retaliates against Iranian missile strikes launched in response to Israeli attack on Beirut
items.summary: The Israeli military has fired back at Iran, with state media reporting explosions in three cities after an Iranian strike on northern Israel this morning.

items.title: When Victoria Police unlawfully disclosed her pregnancy, Joanna Adams took it to VCAT and won
items.summary: A woman who says she suffered life-changing stress and anxiety — and later, the death of one of her babies — after a Victoria Police officer unlawfully disclosed that she was pregnant wants her story known.

items.title: Politicians are challenging party lines on migration, gambling and AUKUS
items.summary: Former Coalition frontbencher Paul Scarr says inflammatory migration rhetoric is taking a toll on migrant communities. His warning comes as both major parties wrestle with populist pressures.

items.title: Anti-immigration sentiment rises in Japan despite growing demand for foreign workers
items.summary: Japan's answer to One Nation skyrockets in popularity after adopting an anti-immigration stance, despite Japan needing foreign workers more than ever.

items.title: Parkinson's disease experts warn watchdog as deadline looms on paraquat decision
items.summary: Australia's leading movement disorder specialists say the chemical regulator has not engaged with their concerns about paraquat, despite growing evidence linking the herbicide to Parkinson's disease.

items.title: Federal government accused of AI policy retreat as US tech giants plan Australian investments
items.summary: The world's biggest tech titans see Australia as a promising investment opportunity for their AI ambitions, but some fear the federal government's regulatory retreat could lead to harm.

items.title: Anthropic sounds the alarm as the AI bubble takes off
items.summary: Anthropic wants caution as AI intelligence surges but the stock market can't get enough.

items.title: Artist Brian Robinson explores everyday magic in Multiverse at Newcastle Art Gallery
items.summary: Newcastle Art Gallery presents the distinctive and intricate work of the Torres Strait Islander artist in its first major solo survey following a four-year refurbishment.

items.title: This week in history quiz, June 8: When was the first Mars Exploration Rover launch?
items.summary: What make of car was stolen in the world's first official vehicle theft? And do you know where the Endeavour was repaired after hitting the Great Barrier Reef in 1770?

items.title: Richard Scolyer dies aged 59, leaving behind legacy of revolutionary brain cancer research
items.summary: The 2024 Australian of the Year Richard Scolyer has died. In his final message he urged all Australians to "pursue their dreams and passion with humility, love and compassion".

items.title: Almost 950 'outstanding' Australians on 2026 King's Birthday Honours list
items.summary: Almost 950 Australians are honoured, including five stand-out recipients who are now Companions of the Order.

items.title: Race Around The World recap: From Alaska to India, catch the week one films
items.summary: From illegal fishing in the Philippines to Michael Jackson impersonators in Las Vegas, catch up on the films from the very first week of Race Around The World.

items.title: ACT government to put some infrastructure projects on backburner to help relieve budget pressure
items.summary: Some ACT government infrastructure projects, including health, emergency services and sports facilities in North Gungahlin, will be deferred to save about $700 million over four years.

items.title: The legacy of Beethoven's Symphony No. 9 and the composers who came after
items.summary: Beethoven's Ninth has topped the ABC Classic 100 countdown for the fifth time. We explore its mystique and shed light on composers following in Beethoven's footsteps.

items.title: Daniel Turpin named as diver killed in shark attack off Albany, WA
items.summary: The family of a 35-year-old diver who died after being bitten by a shark has described him as "an adored husband, son, brother and uncle who had a lifelong love and deep respect for the ocean".

items.title: SA Police investigating after man dies after 'ineffective' taser arrest

items.summary: Carlton continues its resurgence under interim coach Josh Fraser with a five-point victory over Essendon, after Sydney completes a thrilling victory over St Kilda.

items.title: A 'flamingo revolution' vs a Trump resort. Inside Albania's anti-Kushner protests

items.title: Teenagers, male arrested after machete brawl at Melbourne's Flinders Street Station
items.summary: Police say up to 10 people were fighting inside the station on Saturday night before some boarded a train still armed with machetes.

items.title: Worsening wait times and toxic culture at Ambulance Tasmania revealed, as new pay deal struck
items.summary: Average wait times for ambulances in Tasmania have exceeded worst-case scenario projections, despite extra resourcing, with many staff wanting to change careers, new survey results show.

items.title: Love story: Accepting my body is my greatest achievement

items.title: Australia turns its attention to FIFA World Cup after practice games
items.summary: The Socceroos have finished their warm-up games, and now turn their attention to Türkiye in their first World Cup match. So, what have we learned from their friendly games against Mexico and Switzerland?

items.title: Volunteers clean up streets to restore pride in Tablelands town
items.summary: Atherton was once named Queensland's tidiest town, but some residents say the main street has become unsightly. Locals have formed a volunteer clean-up crew to restore the Tablelands town to its former glory.

items.title: Private medical imaging provider bills Medicare for non-eligible services
items.summary: A private medical imaging provider operating in a public hospital has been billing Medicare for some services that are not eligible for bulk billing, according to an internal memo obtained by the ABC.

items.title: Greens senator says Australia cannot defend sea trade lanes, rejecting AUKUS reasoning
items.summary: Greens defence spokesman David Shoebridge says it is "ridiculous" to think Australia's military could defend the nation's global trade lanes, and acquiring nuclear-powered submarines under AUKUS only invites Australia to a conflict between the United States and China.

items.title: How Tasmania's planned new stadium could trigger deal for the Hobart Hurricanes
items.summary: Cricket's smallest state says it could become a major player in privatisation, with a potential Hobart Hurricanes deal tied to Tasmania's planned Macquarie Point roofed stadium.

items.title: Choirs bring community together and offer antidote to loneliness
items.summary: As winter isolation sets in, community choirs are providing connection, belonging and emotional support.

items.title: Boy on bail charged over stabbing of homeless man in Bendigo

items.title: Family remembers Wilson Sprague as passionate stockman and 'good bloke'
items.summary: A pilot and talented stockman, Wilson Sprague's family and friends share their story of the "good bloke", who died in a crash in 2025.

items.title: Multiple people shot near Ohio festival, US authorities say
items.summary: Police are searching for multiple suspects after at least 12 people were injured in a shooting near the Old West End Festival in Toledo, Ohio.

items.title: New technology developed in Canberra could prevent 70 million tonnes of milk waste each year
items.summary: About a sixth of the world's milk is wasted, over 150 million tonnes every year, but a Canberra company has come up with new technology that could drastically reduce that.

items.title: Father of infant boy charged with grievous bodily harm on Sunshine Coast
items.summary: A man is charged after his nine-week-old baby was taken to hospital with life-threatening injuries in April.

items.title: Mirra Andreeva wins French Open after defeating Maja Chwalińska 6-3, 6-2 in women's final
items.summary: Mirra Andreeva defeats surprise finalist Maja Chwalińska 6-3, 6-2 to become the youngest French Open champion in more than three decades.

items.title: India's viral 'cockroach' political movement spills onto Delhi's roads
items.summary: India's largest online youth movement has taken to the streets for the first time, calling for the resignation of the education minister.

items.title: Stacey Marinkovich: Pressure, pride and the pursuit of Commonwealth Games gold

items.title: From 'rain bombs' to El Niño: Debunking Australia's greatest weather myths and misconceptions
items.summary: Meteorological rhetoric in Australia, including from the media, can at times be inaccurate, misconstrued or just completely fictitious.

items.title: How the looksmaxxing social media trend is affecting young men
items.summary: While it's a bit of fun for some, the social media trend known as looksmaxxing can have major impacts on young men's physical and mental health.

items.title: In the great data centre boom, will the benefits flow offshore again?
items.summary: More than $150 billion is lined up for data centre development and investment in Australia, but there are questions as to whether the boom will provide benefits beyond a short-term construction boost.

items.summary: Admit it, you can't get enough of my synonyms and similes. Let's get spelling!

items.title: Ukraine renews attacks on St Petersburg after Putin rejects Zelenskyy meeting
items.summary: A large-scale Ukrainian drone attack targets St Petersburg, with Russia intercepting 376 drones.

items.summary: The ABC understands the funeral of Sydney gangster Lorenzo Lemalu was originally scheduled to take place at the venue in Punchbowl, but the plans were changed.

items.title: Mexico score late winner against sloppy Matildas
items.summary: The lacklustre Matildas have been beaten by Mexico for the first time in over 20 years, losing 1-0 in Newcastle.

items.title: Broncos' season in crisis as Titans young gun conjures golden moment
items.summary: Keano Kini scores one of the great game-winning tries to condemn Brisbane to a fifth-straight defeat, with the Titans snatching a 28-23 victory at Lang Park.

items.title: New Socceroo Cristian Volpato says he was 'a bit too scared' to switch allegiance from Italy
items.summary: New Socceroo Cristian Volpato opens up on his decision to switch allegiance from Italy to Australia ahead of the FIFA World Cup.

items.title: Diver dies after attack by suspected 4.5m shark off Albany

items.title: Karen Read sues US police for alleged misconduct, negligence over boyfriend John O'Keefe's death
items.summary: After being cleared of allegations she hit her police officer boyfriend and left him to die, Karen Read now claims agencies that investigated the incident were riddled with "bigotry, misogyny [and] systemic failures".

items.title: Australia forecast to produce a record 2.2 million tonnes of lentils
items.summary: Australia's winter crop production is expected to be down this year, but lentils are bucking the trend and headed towards a record.

items.title: Power hang on for thrilling win over Eagles, Dockers claim record win
items.summary: Port Adelaide hangs on for a thrilling win over West Coast after Logan Morris kicked seven in a Brisbane QClash win and Fremantle beat North Melbourne by 124 points to claim the biggest win in its history.

items.title: More Australians targeted by Booking.com scams, with travellers lucrative targets for criminals
